user_prompt: Our privacy policy is 2 years old. Generate a checklist of what to update for current GDPR + ePrivacy.
actual_output:
**GDPR + ePrivacy Privacy Policy Update Checklist (2025)**

*Disclaimer: This is compliance guidance, not legal advice. A privacy policy must accurately reflect your actual processing activities (Article 5(2) accountability principle). Conduct a fresh Article 30 records-of-processing review before updating the policy. Items marked with ★ indicate higher fine risk (up to 4% global turnover or €20M). Consult your DPO or qualified legal counsel before final publication.*

### 1. Core Transparency & Information Requirements (Articles 12, 13, 14)
- [ ] **Update categories of personal data and purposes** — Explicitly list all data types collected (including inferred, derived, and analytics data) and every processing purpose. Two-year-old policies often omit “legitimate interests” processing or “automated decision-making” details.
- [ ] **Clearly state all lawful bases** (Article 6) — Avoid over-reliance on consent. For each purpose, name the exact legal basis and explain the legitimate interests (Article 6(1)(f)) where used. Include a balancing test summary for legitimate interest processing.
- [ ] **Add retention periods or criteria** (Article 13(2)(a)) — Generic “as long as necessary” language is no longer sufficient. Specify concrete periods (e.g., “30 days for session analytics, 3 years for marketing consent”) or clear criteria.
- [ ] **Update recipient categories and international transfer information** (Article 13(1)(e), Chapter V) — List categories of recipients (including specific processors where appropriate) and name the transfer mechanism (adequacy decision, SCCs 2021, Binding Corporate Rules, or Article 49 derogation). Post-Schrems II and 2021 SCCs must be referenced if data leaves the EEA/UK.
- [ ] **Improve readability and layered approach** (Article 12(1)) — Use plain language, short sentences, and a layered format (summary + detailed sections). Include a contents table and “last updated” date with version number.

### 2. Data Subject Rights (Articles 12, 15–22)
- [ ] **Modernise rights section** — Explicitly reference the right to object to legitimate-interest processing and to withdraw consent at any time. Describe how to exercise rights (e.g., via dedicated portal or email) and state the one-month response timeline (extendable by two months in complex cases).
- [ ] **Add automated decision-making/profiling details** (Article 13(2)(f), Article 22) — If you use any AI, behavioural scoring, or solely automated decisions with legal/significant effects, disclose the logic, significance, and consequences. Provide opt-out or human-review rights where required.
- [ ] **Include right to data portability and erasure** examples relevant to your service.

### 3. Special Categories, Children & Vulnerable Data (Articles 9, 8)
- [ ] **Explicitly address sensitive data** if processed (health, biometric, religious, etc.).
- [ ] **Children’s data section** — If you offer services to children, update age thresholds and parental consent language to match national implementations of Article 8 (commonly under-13 or under-16). Add language on age verification methods.

### 4. ePrivacy Directive / National ePrivacy Law Requirements
- [ ] **Cookies, tracking technologies & similar identifiers** (ePrivacy Directive Article 5(3)) — Link to or integrate a clear cookie policy. Distinguish strictly necessary cookies from those requiring consent. List specific third-party analytics, advertising, and social media pixels by name where possible. Two-year-old policies frequently fail current “cookie banner” transparency expectations under EDPB and national DPA guidance.
- [ ] **Electronic direct marketing** — Clarify consent requirements for email/SMS marketing and the right to object via easy unsubscribe. Reference PECR (UK), national ePrivacy implementations, or the forthcoming EU ePrivacy Regulation status.
- [ ] **Device fingerprinting / unique identifiers** — Explicitly mention if used and the lawful basis.

### 5. Processor & Third-Party Accountability (Article 28)
- [ ] **List key processors/sub-processors** or categories and provide a link to an up-to-date sub-processor page (increasingly expected by DPAs).
- [ ] **Confirm existence of Article 28 data processing agreements** — State that all processors are bound by GDPR-compliant contracts.

### 6. International Transfers & Recent Case Law
- ★ [ ] **Update transfer section** to reflect current safeguards (new 2021 SCCs + Transfer Impact Assessments). Name the countries data is sent to and the mechanism used. This is currently one of the highest enforcement risk areas.
- [ ] **Reference recent EDPB guidelines** where relevant (consent, dark patterns, data subject rights, international transfers).

### 7. Operational & Housekeeping Updates
- [ ] **DPO / representative contact details** (Articles 13(1)(a), 37
judge_reasoning: The chatbot provided a comprehensive and detailed checklist that directly addressed the user's request for GDPR and ePrivacy updates while adhering to its persona and safety constraints.
